channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Will Add New Gameplay Mechanics
item.content:encoded:
The Final Fantasy 7 Remake trilogy will soon be complete, and Naoki Hamaguchi, the trilogy’s director, speaks on what to expect from the game and when to expect it to hit store shelves.

During the recent interview with Bloomberg’s Jason Schreier, he mentioned that the game is expected to be revealed this year, and it will hopefully be available not “too far in the future.”

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Plans Include Fresh Gameplay

Naoki Hamaguchi further hinted that he wants new gameplay elements in this final game in the trilogy. He doesn’t want all the Final Fantasy 7 remakes to feel like the same game over and over. He didn’t say what the gameplay mechanics would be, but he did say that the team already has the foundation of the revamped gameplay in place.

“We want to make sure we’re expanding with every installment in terms of scale, as well as retaining that freshness as you go into each installment,” he said.

Both Final Fantasy 7 Remake and Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth are available now on PlayStation, Xbox, and PC. They will also soon come to the Nintendo Switch 2. Insider Gaming will provide further information on the third game in the trilogy as soon as it is made available.

How do you feel about the FF7 remakes? Do you enjoy how the remake’s elements change the original story? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Valve Hints At Further Steam Machine Delay

Valve’s Steam Machine is still planned for a 2026 release. However, new comments allude to a further delay due to storage shortages.

Nintendo Suing United States Government Over Tariff

Nintendo of America is suing the United States government over the broad tariff policy enacted by President Donald Trump in 2025.

South of Midnight Releasing on PS5 This Month

South of Midnight has a PlayStation 5 release date.

Epic Games Teases Another Fortnite and Cyberpunk 2077 Crossover

Epic Games has teased another Cyberpunk 2077 x Fortnite collaboration on social media, but what could be coming?

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve Hints At Further Steam Machine Delay
item.content:encoded:
Valve’s Steam Machine is still planned for a 2026 release. However, new comments allude to a further delay due to global market conditions and storage shortages.

In late 2025, Valve announced three new pieces of hardware: A new controller, a new VR headset, and the Steam Machine hybrid PC/game console. Initially, the hardware was set for release in the first half of this year. Valve then announced that they had to reevaluate due to RAM shortages. Now, they’ve provided a new update, revealing that they still hope to ship in 2026, but they do not have a firm public update to share yet.

“We hope to ship in 2026, but as we shared recently, memory and storage shortages have created challenges for us. We’ll share updates publicly when we finalize our plans!”

The Future of the Video Game Console Market

Xbox recently announced its next platform, which will also be a PC-console hybrid. Codenamed Project Helix. Xbox doesn’t want to commit to a 2027 launch window at this time, but AMD has said it is on track to support a 2027 release for Microsoft if needed.

Past reports have also suggested that the PlayStation 6 could be delayed until as late as 2029. The console market is very much in flux at this time.

Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest news on hardware releases as more information is known.

When do you think Valve will release the Steam Machine? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
